articles.title: Rochdale Council Plans Crackdown on Houses of Multiple Occupation
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Council is set to introduce tighter controls on houses of multiple occupation following recent protests in Heywood.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Council is set to crack down on houses of multiple occupation (HMOs) in the borough. Reform councillors have called on town hall officials to implement stricter regulations. This decision follows protests against HMOs that took place over the weekend in Heywood.

Councillor Paul O’Neill stated that the council aims to introduce an Article 4 Direction to alleviate tensions surrounding HMOs. The issue was raised by Reform councillors during the latest town hall meeting. Article 4 directives would require all HMOs, regardless of size, to obtain specific planning permission. Currently, only HMOs accommodating seven or more people require such permission.

Councillor Lee Wolf from Reform expressed concerns that while HMO licensing is important, it does not address the location of these properties or prevent excessive clustering in certain areas. He noted that residents desire a greater say in the matter, as many believe HMOs are impacting local services.

The Labour group, which holds control of the council, indicated that it is already planning changes. Housing lead Councillor Danny Meredith thanked Reform for its support of the Labour plan, while fellow Labour councillor Richard Jackson emphasised the need to follow proper processes before implementing Article 4, stating that evidence is necessary for such a measure. The proposal is currently undergoing local authority processes and is expected to be brought to the full council for a formal vote later this year.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: St. Edmund’s Church in Castleton Dates Back to the 11th Century
articles.excerpt: St. Edmund’s Church, located in Castleton, has historical roots dating back to the 11th and 12th centuries, showcasing its significance in the area.
articles.content_html:
St. Edmund’s Church in Castleton has historical origins that date back to the 11th and 12th centuries. This church is a notable landmark in the area, reflecting the architectural and cultural heritage of the time.

The church has served the local community for centuries, playing a vital role in the spiritual life of Castleton.

Visitors to St. Edmund’s can appreciate its historical significance and the craftsmanship of its early architecture.

As a part of Castleton's heritage, St. Edmund’s Church continues to be a focal point for community events and gatherings.

articles.title: Average Asking Rent in Rochdale Reaches Record High
articles.excerpt: The average asking rent in Rochdale has reached a record high, reflecting ongoing trends in the housing market.
articles.content_html:
A recent report indicates that the average asking rent in Rochdale has reached a record high. This development highlights the increasing demand for rental properties in the area.

The rise in rental prices is attributed to various factors, including a shortage of available properties and growing interest in the region.

Local residents may find that this increase in rental costs could impact their housing options, making it more challenging to secure affordable accommodation.

articles.title: Rochdale Students Win National Award for Online Safety Film
articles.excerpt: Students from Skills 4 All in Rochdale have secured second place in the secondary category of the 2026 Childnet Film Competition with a film on the responsible use of artificial intelligence.
articles.content_html:
A group of students from Skills 4 All in Rochdale has won second place in the secondary category of the 2026 Childnet Film Competition. Their film addresses the safe and responsible use of artificial intelligence (AI).

The awards were announced at Disney headquarters in Hammersmith during the 17th annual Childnet Film Competition, which invites young people across the UK to create short films promoting online safety. This year's theme was 'Smart tech, safe choices', focusing on the implications of AI in everyday life. The students' film is set in a dystopian version of Manchester, illustrating the potential consequences of over-reliance on AI.

The judging panel, which included representatives from the BBC, the British Board of Film Classification, Disney, and the Motion Picture Association, commended the film for its creativity and engaging storytelling. As runners-up, the students will receive professional filmmaking equipment, and their film will be classified by the BBFC, allowing it to be screened in cinemas across the UK.

Will Gardner OBE, chief executive of Childnet, highlighted the importance of educating young people about the responsible use of AI, noting that 97% of children aged 8 to 17 have used AI in some form. Charlotte Tudor, vice president of marketing for Disney, expressed pride in supporting the initiative, emphasising the need for safe online experiences.
articles.legal_disclaimer: This article was compiled from identified public sources and may be updated when further verified information becomes available.

articles.title: Rochdale Valiant Launches New Media Portal
articles.excerpt: Rochdale Valiant has unveiled a new media portal aimed at providing news and information for the community. The platform is designed to engage residents with timely updates and local stories.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale Valiant has launched a new media portal, aimed at delivering news and information to the community. The platform, described as a resource for a new generation, seeks to engage residents with timely updates and local stories.

The portal features various sections, including news articles and community announcements, designed to keep residents informed about local developments and events.

According to the announcement, the initiative aims to enhance public protection through published media, ensuring that important information reaches the community effectively.

Residents can access the new portal at Rochdale Valiant's website, where they can find the latest news and updates relevant to Rochdale and its surrounding areas.

articles.title: Self-Employed Driver Launches New Service in Rochdale
articles.excerpt: A self-employed driver has launched a new service in Rochdale, offering residents an alternative transport option. The service aims to cater to those seeking flexible travel arrangements within the area. The driver has been operating in Rochdale for several years and plans to provide reliable and affordable transport solutions.
articles.content_html:
A self-employed driver has launched a new service in Rochdale, offering residents an alternative transport option. The service aims to cater to those seeking flexible travel arrangements within the area.

The driver has been operating in Rochdale for several years and plans to provide reliable and affordable transport solutions.

Further details about the service, including pricing and availability, can be obtained directly from the driver. Residents interested in utilising the service are encouraged to reach out for more information.
